items.name: ShinyHunters
items.targets: Hundreds of Salesforce customers in new data theft campaign targeting Salesforce Experience Cloud configurations; threatened 400 firms over stolen data
items.ttps: T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application), T1213 (Data from Information Repositories) - Modified AuraInspector tool used to mass-scan and harvest data from overly permissive Salesforce Cloud configurations

items.name: Lazarus (North Korea)
items.targets: AllSecure CEO targeted via fake LinkedIn interview; Gogs vulnerability exploitation for LFS object manipulation
items.ttps: T1566.001 (Phishing: Spearphishing Attachment), T1204.002 (User Execution: Malicious File) - Social engineering via fake job interviews and legitimate platform compromise

items.name: MuddyWater (Iranian APT)
items.targets: U.S. critical infrastructure and networks; multiple U.S. firms targeted with new Dindoor backdoor malware
items.ttps: T1071 (Application Layer Protocol), T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter), T1105 (Ingress Tool Transfer) - Custom Dindoor backdoor deployment, persistence mechanisms

items.name: APT28 (Russia, Fancy Bear)
items.targets: Ukrainian military forces in long-term espionage operations; Signal and WhatsApp accounts of government officials globally
items.ttps: T1566 (Phishing), T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter), T1027 (Obfuscated Files) - Advanced custom malware toolkit revival, cyber-espionage operations, messaging app compromise

items.name: Iranian MOIS Actors
items.targets: Medtech firm Stryker (claimed wiper attack); U.S. and Middle East targets as part of psychological cyber warfare including fake 'Shelter Danger' calls
items.ttps: T1485 (Data Destruction), T1498 (Network Denial of Service), T1566.001 (Phishing) - Wiper malware deployment, psychological operations, phishing campaigns tied to Iran-Israel conflict

items.name: Qilin Ransomware
items.targets: U.S. electric cooperative (purported breach); RWB Consulting Engineers
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery) - Double extortion ransomware attacks targeting critical infrastructure

items.name: Akira Ransomware
items.targets: netCOMPONENTS and multiple organizations per dark web activity
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery) - Data exfiltration and encryption operations

items.name: Everest Ransomware
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1048 (Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol) - Ransomware deployment with data theft

items.name: Chinese Nexus Actors
items.targets: Qatar government and organizations amid Iranian conflict; PlugX malware lures tied to Middle East conflict
items.ttps: T1566.001 (Phishing: Spearphishing Attachment), T1204 (User Execution), T1027 (Obfuscated Files) - PlugX RAT deployment with geopolitical lures

items.name: Scattered Spider (UNC3944)
items.targets: Listed among worst active ransomware groups; ongoing operations
items.ttps: T1078 (Valid Accounts), T1566.002 (Phishing: Spearphishing Link), T1110 (Brute Force) - Social engineering, credential theft, and ransomware deployment

items.name: BeatBanker
items.targets: Android users via dual-mode trojan with phishing capabilities
items.ttps: T1417 (Input Capture), T1533 (Data from Local System), T1566.001 (Phishing) - Banking trojan with overlay attacks and SMS interception

items.name: SurxRAT
items.targets: Android users; uses LLMs for automated phishing and data theft
items.ttps: T1417 (Input Capture), T1636 (Protected User Data), T1566 (Phishing) - AI-enhanced phishing with RAT capabilities

items.name: GhostClaw / GhostLoader
items.targets: Developers via fake OpenClaw npm package masquerading as legitimate tools
items.ttps: T1195.002 (Supply Chain Compromise: Compromise Software Supply Chain), T1027 (Obfuscated Files), T1059.007 (JavaScript) - Malicious npm package distribution targeting developer environments

items.name: A0Backdoor Operators
items.targets: Organizations via Microsoft Teams social engineering and fake Zoom/Teams apps with signed malware
items.ttps: T1566.001 (Phishing: Spearphishing Attachment), T1036.005 (Masquerading: Match Legitimate Name), T1219 (Remote Access Software) - Novel backdoor distributed via Teams phishing, drops RMM tools

items.name: Tycoon 2FA
items.targets: Phishing-as-a-Service platform disrupted by law enforcement
items.ttps: T1566.002 (Phishing: Spearphishing Link), T1539 (Steal Web Session Cookie), T1056.003 (Input Capture: Web Portal Capture) - AitM phishing kit for AWS account hijacking and credential theft

items.name: INC Ransom
items.targets: Pacific networks including Australia, New Zealand, and Tonga
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery), T1562.001 (Impair Defenses) - Rising ransomware attacks across Pacific region

canada_landscape.summary: Canadian entities face elevated risk from international threat actors. Phishing campaigns leveraging .arpa domain abuse and Canadian-themed social engineering have been detected. Meta disabled 150K accounts linked to Southeast Asia scam centers affecting Canadian victims. Dark web monitoring revealed alleged Canada data listings alongside AT&T and Canva breaches.
canada_landscape.retailers: None identified - no specific incidents involving Canadian Tire, SportChek, Mark's, or other major Canadian retailers reported in the last 48 hours
canada_landscape.financial: None identified - no specific incidents involving Canadian financial institutions (CIBC, RBC, TD Bank, Scotiabank, BMO) reported in the last 48 hours, though general data listings mentioning 'Canada' appeared on dark web forums
